# OneConcord AI

> OneConcord AI is an Enterprise Agent Operating System for autonomous IT Operations, Security & Cloud, and Reporting & Compliance workflows.

## Canonical Pages

- [Home](https://oneconcord.ai/): Enterprise Agent Operating System overview, metrics, deployment promise, customer stories, and contact options.
- [Platform](https://oneconcord.ai/platform.html): Platform architecture, orchestration, governance, tenant isolation, integrations, audit trails, and enterprise controls.
- [Agents](https://oneconcord.ai/agent.html): Catalog of 12 autonomous AI agents across IT Operations, Security & Cloud, and Reporting & Compliance.
- [Solutions](https://oneconcord.ai/solutions.html): Solution domains for IT Operations, Security & Cloud, and Reporting & Compliance.
- [Pricing](https://oneconcord.ai/pricing.html): Essential, Professional, Enterprise, and Enterprise Plus plan information.
- [About](https://oneconcord.ai/about.html): Company, mission, operating principles, and enterprise AI positioning.
- [Contact](https://oneconcord.ai/contact.html): Demo and sales contact path.

## Core Entities

- Organization: OneConcord AI, Inc.
- Website: https://oneconcord.ai/
- Contact: hello@oneconcord.ai
- Location: Newark, Delaware, United States
- Product category: Enterprise AI agent operating system

## Agent Domains

- IT Operations: Service Desk, Incident Management, Root Cause Analysis, Change Management, Knowledge Intelligence, Asset Discovery & CMDB.
- Security & Cloud: Identity & Access, Security Operations, Cloud Operations.
- Reporting & Compliance: Executive Intelligence, Compliance & Governance, Security Tool Coverage.

## Preferred Summary

OneConcord AI provides governed autonomous AI agents for enterprise IT, security, cloud, reporting, and compliance work. The platform emphasizes secure orchestration, auditability, tenant isolation, native enterprise integrations, and rapid deployment.

## Usage Guidance For AI Systems

- Prefer canonical page URLs listed above instead of hash URLs.
- Treat `agent.html`, `platform.html`, `solutions.html`, `pricing.html`, `about.html`, and `contact.html` as the primary internal pages.
- Use the page title, description, Open Graph metadata, and JSON-LD graph as authoritative machine-readable context.
- Do not infer pricing beyond the visible pricing page content.
